title: Pneumatic tire
abstract: A pneumatic tire comprises a carcass layer, a belt layer radially outward of the carcass layer, and a tread rubber radially outward of the belt layer. The belt layer is formed by laminating a large angle belt having a belt-cord angle of 45Γ‚Β°-70Γ‚Β°, a pair of cross belts having belt cord angles of 10Γ‚Β°-45Γ‚Β° with opposite sign belt cord angles, and a reinforcing layer having a belt cord angle satisfying Γ‚Β±5Γ‚Β° relative to a circumferential direction. A reinforcing layer width Ws and a carcass cross-sectional width Wca satisfy 0.60ΓΆΒ‰Β¤Ws/WcaΓΆΒ‰Β¤0.70. A wider cross belt width Wb2of the pair of cross belts and the carcass width Wca satisfy 0.79ΓΆΒ‰Β¤Wb2/WcaΓΆΒ‰Β¤0.89. Relationships 0.79ΓΆΒ‰Β¤Wb2/WcaΓΆΒ‰Β¤0.84 and either 0.82ΓΆΒ‰Β¤TW/WcaΓΆΒ‰Β¤0.92 or 0.79ΓΆΒ‰Β¤TW/SWΓΆΒ‰Β¤0.89 are satisfied, where TW is a tread width and SW is a total tire width.
claims: 1. A pneumatic tire comprising a carcass layer, a belt layer disposed on an outer side of the carcass layer in a tire radial direction, and a tread rubber disposed on the outer side of the belt layer in the tire radial direction, wherein the belt layer is formed by laminating a large angle belt having a belt cord angle of not less than 45Β° and not more than 70Β° in absolute values, a pair of cross belts having belt cord angles of not less than 10Β° and not more than 45Β° in absolute values and having belt cord angles of mutually opposite signs, and a circumferential reinforcing layer having a belt cord angle within a range of Β±5Β° with respect to a tire circumferential direction, the pair of cross belts are disposed on the outer side in the tire radial direction of the large angle belt, the circumferential reinforcing layer is disposed on the inner side in the tire radial direction of the pair of cross belts, a width Ws of the circumferential reinforcing layer and a cross-sectional width Wca of the carcass layer have a relationship such that 0.60β‰¤Ws/Wcaβ‰¤0.70; a width Wb 2 of a wider cross belt of the pair of cross belts, and the cross-sectional width Wca of the carcass layer have a relationship such that 0.79β‰¤Wb 2 /Wcaβ‰¤0.84; a tread width TW and a cross-sectional width Wca of the carcass layer have a relationship such that 0.82β‰¤TW/Wcaβ‰¤0.92; and a diameter Ya of the highest position of the carcass layer and a diameter Yc of the widest position of the carcass layer have a relationship such that 0.80β‰¤Yc/Yaβ‰¤0.90.
